etymology.interpretation: 數(數) (shù, "number") represents counting or measurable quantities, combining with 字(字) (zì, "character; word"), which refers to written symbols or units of language. Together, the word captures the idea of numerals as characters that represent numbers and are the graphical units for expressing numerical quantities.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢数学，因为我喜欢研究数字(我喜歡數學，因為我喜歡研究數字)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān shùxué, yīnwèi wǒ xǐhuān yánjiū shùzì.
examples.english: I like mathematics because I enjoy studying numbers.

examples.chinese: 密码由六个数字组成(密碼由六個數字組成)。
examples.pinyin: Mìmǎ yóu liù gè shùzì zǔchéng.
examples.english: The password consists of six numbers.

examples.chinese: 我不太擅长记数字(我不太擅長記數字)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bù tài shàncháng jì shùzì.
examples.english: I am not very good at remembering numbers.
